stories.title: Italy allowed US planes to use bases in Iran war, NATO’s Rutte says
stories.summary: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Rome allowed 500 U.S. military aircraft to take off from American bases in Italy during U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran, sparking a political backlash in Italy. Speaking to Fox News late Tuesday ahead of a meeting with U.S. President Donald Trump in Washington, Rutte also said: “Country after country, ally after ally … have made their bases available … That means thousands, between 4,000 and 5,000 … planes taking off from European bases to support Ep

stories.title: Czech constitutional court says president should attend NATO summit
stories.summary: The Czech Republic’s Constitutional Court issued a preliminary injunction on Wednesday allowing President Petr Pavel to attend the July NATO summit in Ankara. The court’s ruling partially resolves the ongoing dispute between Pavel and Prime Minister Andrej Babiš, who announced on Monday that Pavel could not be part of the country’s delegation. Pavel filed a competency lawsuit the following day, seeking clarity on who has the authority to decide whether the head of state may att

stories.title: European leaders pledge stronger role in NATO before Trump-Rutte talks
stories.summary: BERLIN — The leaders of Germany, France, Italy, the U.K. and Poland pledged to strengthen NATO’s European pillar ahead of a Wednesday meeting between the chief of the alliance and U.S. President Donald Trump. The leaders of the so-called E5 group met in Berlin to coordinate a joint position ahead of next month’s NATO summit in Ankara. They also discussed support for Ukraine as Russia faces setbacks on the battlefield and as Trump, after months focused largely on Iran, pledged increased bac

stories.title: Israeli former leaders and security chiefs threaten legal action over ‘Jewish terrorism’
stories.summary: Leaked letter to PM and military demands action to stop violence against Palestinians in occupied West Bank Dozens of Israelis from the country’s security, political and cultural elite have threatened legal action against their government over support for Jewish terrorism and an “ideology of ethnic cleansing” in the occupied West Bank , according to a leaked letter. Two former prime ministers, former heads of all the Israeli security services, former judges, a Nobel laureate and the country’s mo

stories.title: Ex-Dutch defense minister named EEAS secretary general
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Former Dutch Defense Minister Kajsa Ollongren has been appointed secretary general of the European External Action Service, the EU’s foreign policy wing. The move, announced in an email to staff on Wednesday afternoon and then made public , reflects the growing importance of defense-related matters in Brussels. It also puts an end to weeks of speculation after predecessor Belén Martínez Carbonell was moved to lead the EU’s delegation in Mexico after two years in the post, as first rep

stories.title: A decade after the Brexit vote, Europe has moved on even if Britain hasn’t
stories.summary: In this week’s newsletter: As the EU consolidates,​ t​he UK faces renewed debate ​over the long‑term shape of its relationship with the continent • Don’t get This Is Europe delivered to your inbox? Sign up here The morning of 24 June 2016, the day after Britain voted to leave the EU, dawned grey and overcast in Brussels, after a stormy night. As the Guardian’s correspondent in the city, after a few hours’ sleep, I hurried to a breakfast briefing with Conservative MEPs at a smart hotel in the EU

stories.title: The Guardian view on priorities for a new prime minister: foreign policy cannot be an afterthought | Editorial
stories.summary: Keir Starmer came to power without a strategic concept of post-Brexit Britain’s place in the world. His successor must not repeat that mistake Sir Keir Starmer had years in opposition to prepare for government. His likely successor, Andy Burnham, has weeks. Unlike the outgoing prime minister, Mr Burnham will bring past ministerial experience to the top job as well as lessons learned as the mayor of Greater Manchester. But as every veteran of No 10 attests, the pressures in that bu

stories.title: Germany news: Major warship project to be scrapped
stories.summary: The Defense Ministry is dropping a multi-billion-euro project to build F126 frigates, media say. And train services have resumed after a communications glitch overnight halted Germany's rail network . DW has more.

stories.title: Seal at Hungarian zoo brushes teeth
stories.summary: A seal at Nyíregyháza Zoo in Hungary brushes its teeth in the Arctic Ice World exhibit, showcasing daily care and animal enrichment activities.

stories.title: Elkezdtük figyelni, miből élünk meg időskorunkban – ez az eredmény
stories.summary: Minden olyan befektetés növekszik, amibe kifejezetten az időskori megélhetés érdekében tesznek pénzt a magyarok. Hosszú évek lemorzsolódása után újra emelkedik az önkéntes nyugdíjpénztárak taglétszáma, tízezrek léptek be ezekbe a kasszákba. Hasonló gyarapodás látható a nyugdíjbiztosításoknál és nyugdíj-előtakarékossági számláknál is. Mintha most már több embernek lenne fontos, hogy miből fog megélni időskorában.

stories.title: Költségvetési csalás gyanújával nyomoznak a Pesti Magyar Színház ügyében
stories.summary: A NAV nyomoz az ügyben ismeretlen tettes ellen.

stories.title: A saláta napoknak vége
stories.summary: Mac DeMarco az antipopsztár, aki mintha véletlenül tévedt volna a Budapest Park színpadára a kukásnadrágjában. Az előadó egy letűnt korszak szimbóluma, a koncertje azonban mégsem a nosztalgiáról szólt.

stories.title: Későbbre tolódott a magyarországi túlfogyasztás napja, de nem azért, mert jobban figyelünk a környezetre
stories.summary: Hanem valószínűleg inkább azért, mert visszaesett itthon az ipari termelés.

stories.title: Egyre több elpusztult delfint találnak a román tengerparton, drónrobbanás is felmerült az okok között
stories.summary: A Fekete-tenger ukrán partjain is vannak hasonló esetek, emiatt érthető, hogy a romániai kutatók számításba veszik a háborús eredetű zajokat és robbanásokat.

stories.title: Ugyanannyi zajt okoz Zuglóban egy landoló repülő, mint a ferihegyi reptér melletti utcákban
stories.summary: A XIV. kerületi önkormányzat nemrég üzemelt be egy zajmérőt, így láthatók az adatok.

stories.title: Elutasították Havasi Bertalan Magyar Péter ellen tett feljelentését, de nem adja fel
stories.summary: Hivatali visszaélés miatt tett feljelentést Havasi, de az ügyészség szerint nem követett el bűncselekményt a miniszterelnök, amikor szóváltásba keveredett a korábbi helyettes államtitkárral.

stories.title: Indítványozták az NKA komplett döntéshozó testületének a letartóztatását
stories.summary: Emellett Bús Balázs, a Nemzeti Kulturális Alap pályázatkezelő szervének főigazgatója és a főigazgató kabinetfőnöke is letartóztatásba kerülhet, ha a bíróság úgy dönt. A hat gyanúsítottból csak hárman tettek vallomást.

stories.title: Az euróbevezetés egyetlen feltételét sem teljesíti Magyarország a friss konvergenciajelentés alapján
stories.summary: Az Európai Bizottság konvergenciajelentéséből látszik, mekkora feladat lesz az új kormánynak, hogy 2030-ra felkészítse az országot az EU pénznemének bevezetésére.

stories.title: Folytatódik a zászlóügy: magyar zászlókkal rakták tele a mi hazánkosok az Erzsébet hidat
stories.summary: Vasárnap este egy magányos, 58 éves férfi dobálta a Dunába a Pride-zászlókat, szerdán már pártaktivisták akcióztak csoportosan.

stories.title: Magyar: Regardless of bilateral differences, the V4 must function
stories.summary: Following the meeting of the V4, we asked the Hungarian PM about the Beneš Decrees, the migration pact, Ukraine’s EU accession, and the constitutional amendment proposed this week.

stories.title: Mészáros Lőrinc inkább a hatvanpusztai zebrákat eteti, mint a Pesti Srácokat
stories.summary: Folytatódnak a leépítések a fideszes médiában, nem látni a NER-es milliárdosokat, akik kisegítenék a közpénz nélkül megtántorodó szerkesztőségeket. Talán érdemes lett volna az olvasókért újságot írni, Orbán Viktor és Mészáros Lőrinc ugyanis nem látszanak hálásnak.

stories.title: A szivárványos lobogók mellett magyar zászlók is díszítik majd az Erzsébet hidat a Mi Hazánk akciója után
stories.summary: Karácsony Gergely szerint úgy tűnik, néhányan mindenképp nemzeti ünnepet akarnak csinálni a Pride-ból.

stories.title: Bosznia-Hercegovina–Katar 2-0
stories.summary: Fél óra kellett Boszniának a vezetés megszerzéséhez, meglehet a továbbjutás.

stories.title: Egy magyar gyerek lezuhant a bledi vár melletti kilátónál, súlyosan megsérült
stories.summary: Katonai helikopterrel szállították Ljubljanába.

stories.title: Canadian police warn of possible copycat attacks after deadly shootout in Montreal
stories.summary: Assailant behind shooting that left three people dead wrote ‘incel’-like manifesto that was posted by a far-right outlet Police in Canada are warning of possible copycat attacks after three people died in a shootout in Montreal and the assailant’s lengthy manifesto, which called for “a new bloodletting”, was posted online by a far-right outlet. The document contains many of the hallmark grievances of the “involuntary celibacy” – or “incel” – movement in addition racist and misogynistic conspirac

stories.title: Poland, Ukraine: Dispute between two close allies deepens
stories.summary: Polish President Karol Nawrocki last week stripped Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of Poland's highest state honor. How did it come to this? And what has it got to do with next year's election in Poland?

stories.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
stories.summary: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.

stories.title: EU looks at tech to bulk up its police agency
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Union wants to make Europol the central nervous system for police to trade data and use cutting-edge technologies, as part of plans to beef up the bloc’s internal security . The European Commission unveiled plans on Wednesday to strengthen the way its law enforcement agency based in The Hague fights crime, as the bloc seeks to formulate a tougher response to threats ranging from cyberattacks to hybrid aggression previously linked to Russia-backed actors. “We a

stories.title: Bahraini award to UK envoy shows ‘our diplomats are up for grabs’, says peer
stories.summary: Rights activists call UK ambassador ‘morally compromised’ for accepting honour from Bahraini king in apparent breach of Foreign Office rules The British ambassador to Bahrain has been accused of breaching government rules over accepting an award by the Gulf state’s king, a move critics suggest signals diplomats and civil servants are “up for grabs”. This week, the ambassador, Alastair Long , received the Order of Bahrain from King Hamad bin Isa al-Khalifa, in recognition of his diplomatic tenure

stories.title: Labor tax critic deletes anti-immigration AI video reposted from rightwing nationalist account
stories.summary: Fund manager Geoff Wilson says he did not watch full video and deleted it after ‘inappropriate associations were identified’ Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The fund manager Geoff Wilson, a prominent public critic of the government’s tax changes, has deleted an inflammatory AI-generated video he reposted from a rightwing nationalist account portraying Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers taking money from white Australians and giving it to recently arrived migrants wear

stories.title: Brazilian federal police arrest Spanish citizen at São Paulo airport for racism
stories.summary: Woman detained at airport after allegedly making racist remarks directed at workers unloading baggage, police say Brazil’s federal police have detained a Spanish citizen in São Paulo’s international Guarulhos airport for racism, in the latest of a series of high-profile arrests of foreign tourists on similar grounds. Brazil has some of the strictest anti-racism laws in Latin America. Insulting a person on the basis of race carries a penalty of imprisonment from two to five years and a fine. Cont

stories.title: What does British PM's resignation mean for European security?
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer was widely known for his support of Ukraine and his efforts towards a new EU–UK defense partnership. What will happen to those policies now that Starmer has announced his resignation?

stories.title: Biggest city in Crimea without power after Ukraine strikes
stories.summary: Sevastopol's Moscow-installed governor warns there will be no electricity in some areas until the evening.

stories.title: Trump’s Gaza Board of Peace will hit the reset button in Cyprus
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump’s Gaza Board of Peace is set to convene at a Cyprus resort on June 30 to “adjust its strategy,” an official familiar with the matter told POLITICO. Two senior EU officials, involved in the arrangements for the gathering and granted anonymity to speak freely, confirmed the meeting will take place on the Mediterranean island next week and last two or three days. The goal is to “reset” after “the Iran war has completely shifted the attention

stories.title: Silvio Berlusconi’s ‘bunga-bunga’ villa sold to Qatari royal family
stories.summary: One of Italy’s most notorious pieces of political real estate has reportedly found a buyer. Qatar’s former Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Hamad bin Jassim bin Jabr Al Thani has acquired Villa Certosa, the Sardinian estate of late Italian Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi, for €350 million , according to Italian daily La Repubblica. The sale comes roughly a year after Berlusconi’s family put the property on the market . The villa overlooking the Gulf of Marinella served as the Ita

stories.title: Rookie One Nation MP blames lack of staff for mistakenly voting with Greens
stories.summary: Opponents seize on Farrer MP David Farley’s decision to vote alongside teals and Greens to wind back fuel tax credits for miners, farmers and others Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast One Nation’s rookie MP David Farley has blamed his political inexperience for mistakenly voting in support of winding back fuel tax credits, which drew condemnation from miners and the Coalition. Farley sided with the Greens and teal i

stories.title: Why off-duty cops in second jobs ‘kill and die more’ in recession-hit Argentina
stories.summary: Growing number of cases involve police working as rideshare drivers while carrying government-issued guns When the gap between his salary and his family’s basic expenses began widening dramatically, Diego – like many other Argentinians – started working as a rideshare driver on top of his day job. He usually does a few hours at the end of his 12-hour shift; and more on his days off. It would be just another story from recession-ridden Argentina, but for the fact that Diego is a federal police of

stories.title: US says chemical giant Chemours to pay $450m to settle ‘forever chemicals’ case
stories.summary: Agreement is first by federal government to resolve enforcement claims against a major Pfas manufacturer The Trump administration on Wednesday reached a multi-state settlement with the chemical giant Chemours Co over years-long, illegal discharges of synthetic “forever chemicals” used to make products resistant to water, grease and stains. The settlement is the first by the federal government to resolve enforcement claims against a manufacturer of harmful chemicals known as Pfas. Under the agree

stories.title: Germany’s railways grind to halt as IT maintenance snag takes down network
stories.summary: Deutsche Bahn widely criticised after hundreds of thousands of passengers stranded in operator’s latest setback Germany’s rail network ground to a halt late on Tuesday as a result of maintenance work that went wrong, leaving hundreds of thousands of passengers unable to get home as the national operator faced widespread criticism over the chaos. The Deutsche Bahn (DB) meltdown was initially thought to have been caused by a cyber-attack, but it later emerged that it was likely to have been trigge

stories.title: Israel says IDF is staying in southern Lebanon, undermining Iran peace talks
stories.summary: Defence minister says troops not withdrawing though Tehran sees end to war in Lebanon as part of deal with US The Israeli defence minister, Israel Katz, has said that Israeli troops would not withdraw from southern Lebanon, further complicating Iran peace talks as fighting in Lebanon continues to be an obstacle to permanent peace. Speaking on stage in an interview in Tel Aviv, Katz said Israeli troops would remain in south Lebanon – echoing sentiments from the Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Ne

stories.title: Pakistan police rescue French woman and children allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years
stories.summary: Husband arrested after Sylvie Yasmina, 54, and five children found at home in north-western province Pakistan police say they have rescued a French woman and her five children after she told authorities she had been held captive by her husband for more than a decade and subjected to years of domestic abuse in the country’s north-west. The woman, identified as 54-year-old Sylvie Yasmina, was rescued earlier this week from a mud-brick home in Bara, a town in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province near the Af

stories.title: Senior No 10 aides to lobby Andy Burnham to invest more than £13.5bn defence spend
stories.summary: Downing Street figures plan to convince Burnham to revive ‘war bonds’ when he becomes prime minister Downing Street aides are planning to lobby Andy Burnham during access talks to revive the idea of “war bonds” to pay for higher defence spending when he becomes prime minister, the Guardian understands. Senior No 10 figures want the Treasury to be allowed to borrow more for military spending and will try to convince Burnham to invest beyond the £13.5bn earmarked for the long-awaited Defence Inves

stories.title: Shabana Mahmood’s immigration and asylum bill to go before MPs next week
stories.summary: Refugee charities fear controversial changes, including on forced removals and age checks, are being rushed through Shabana Mahmood’s controversial plans to increase the forced removal of people refused asylum, introduce stringent age checks for people claiming to be children and limit applications under human rights laws are scheduled to be placed before MPs within days. The immigration and asylum bill is expected to be put before parliament next Tuesday and will face opposition from some Labou

stories.title: China detains two Japanese nationals on smuggling charges
stories.summary: China has detained two Japanese nationals on suspicion of smuggling banned goods. Reports suggest that at least one case may involve strategic rare-earth-related exports.

stories.title: North Korean soldier crosses border to South
stories.summary: South Korea's military says it is investigating the soldier's reasons for crossing the inner-Korean border. While dozens of North Koreans flee each year, usually via China, crossing directly south is very rare.

stories.title: Trump offers Iran an economic lifeline — with strings
stories.summary: Iran has seen a speedy rebound in oil exports after the US-Iran deal granted immediate waivers on US sanctions. Relief on frozen assets and a $300 billion reconstruction fund now hinge on Tehran meeting key conditions.

stories.title: Iran after the war — fragile peace, shattered economy
stories.summary: The US has temporarily allowed Iran to export oil and indicated it will provide billions to relieve the crisis triggered by the war. With ordinary Iranians at their limit, however, Tehran already fears new protests.

stories.title: Colombia's left-wing presidential candidate concedes defeat
stories.summary: Senator Iván Cepeda was beaten by right-wing businessman Abelardo de la Espriella by less than a percentage point.

stories.title: UK prioritised ties with UAE over averting mass atrocities in Sudan, MPs to be told
stories.summary: Foreign Office failed to act on warnings of genocide due to ‘pressure’ from emirates, Yale human rights investigator will tell a parliamentary select committee The British government had received intelligence that Ethiopia appeared to be supporting a genocidal militia in Sudan’s civil war as far back as 2024 but did not go public with the news for fear of upsetting the United Arab Emirates (UAE), a parliamentary committee will hear. In May 2024, officials from the Foreign, Commonwealth and Devel

stories.title: Ukraine says major Crimea bridge destroyed in latest attack
stories.summary: Ukraine continues to hit infrastructure on the Crimea to isolate the Russian-annexed peninsula. Meanwhile, Russia attacked Volodymyr Zelenskyy's hometown with a ballistic missile in a deadly strike.

stories.title: Western Australian police to review response to Virginia Giuffre domestic violence dispute
stories.summary: Police will investigate how they interacted with Giuffre, one of Jeffrey Epstein’s most prominent victims, after her family requested the review Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Police in Western Australia have agreed to review how they interacted with Virginia Giuffre, one of the most prominent victims of the disgraced US financier Jeffrey Epstein , in the lead-up to her death a year ago by suicide. Giuffre’s brother and sister-in-law, Sky and Amanda Roberts, told AB

stories.title: Australian citizen working as spy for Iran ‘orchestrated’ Bondi firebombing, Asio boss says
stories.summary: Mike Burgess’s annual threat assessment warns social media is ‘amplifying’ an erosion of trust in institutions, promoting discord and heightening polarisation Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An Australian citizen working as a senior intelligence officer for Iran “orchestrated” a firebombing in Bondi, the country’s top spy has claimed, while a former Australian resident in Iraq allegedly directed an attack on a Melbourne synagogue. The alleged homegrown ties to Austra

stories.title: Philippines blocks GoreBox gaming app after school shooting kills three
stories.summary: Investigators says one of two teenagers who allegedly opened fire on students at high school was regular player Philippine authorities have temporarily blocked the online gaming app GoreBox days after a rare school shooting in the south-east Asian country killed three students and injured 20 others. Investigators said that one of the two teenagers accused of opening fire on students at San Jose National high school in Tacloban city had regularly played the game, which allows players to use vario

stories.title: France confirms first Ebola case in doctor who had worked in DRC
stories.summary: French health ministry says patient’s contacts are being traced and that risk to European public is very low The first case of Ebola has been confirmed in France, the country’s health ministry has said, in a doctor who had returned from a humanitarian mission to an area affected by the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo. The patient was transferred to a specialist facility and was in a stable condition, the ministry said in a statement . “All precautionary measures, including the p

stories.title: ‘It’s defiance’: why some Bosniaks are reviving historic flag as they cheer on World Cup squad
stories.summary: Lilies making a vivid comeback alongside foreign-imposed flag, as younger generation wrestles with national identity Before each game in Bosnia and Herzegovina’s World Cup adventure, Sarajevo has blossomed with primary colours – and two distinct flags. One is the national flag dating to 1998: blue and yellow diagonal halves emblazoned with a slanting line of white stars. The other has golden lilies on a blue shield set against a white background, and has a far deeper history, steeped in centurie

stories.title: German swimming lake criticised for ban on non-German speakers
stories.summary: Policy at Heidesee lake in Halle introduced after cases in which visitors ignored rules and lifeguards’ instructions An open air swimming lake in the eastern German city of Halle which has refused entry to bathers who don’t speak German has been told it must lift the ban or face possible legal action. The Heidesee lake, a lake in a flooded former open-cast mine, recently introduced a check at the entrance to filter out visitors whose German was deemed not good enough to follow safety instruction

stories.title: The pianist from the audience at La La Land in Concert was a global feelgood story – but all was not what it seemed
stories.summary: Two orchestra members from the Sydney performance allege tensions behind the scenes were the cause of pianist failing to appear after intermission Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast When an audience member stepped in for a pianist at a Sydney performance of La La Land in Concert last month it made global headlines. During the intermission of the concert at Darling Harbour Theatre on 30 May, the two-time Oscar-winning composer and conductor Justin Hurwitz appealed to the

stories.title: Great Britain’s grid operator pays £10m for extra power to avoid supply crunch tonight
stories.summary: Neso expected to pay millions to secure enough electricity to meet demand as households turn on fans and air conditioning Great Britain’s grid operator is expected to pay millions to fire up gas power plants to avoid a rare summer power supply crunch on Wednesday evening as extreme heat puts pressure on the energy system. The National Energy System Operator (Neso) is expected to pay about £10m on Wednesday to secure enough electricity to meet demand as households turn on air conditioners and ele

stories.title: Colombia’s leftwing candidate concedes election to Trump-endorsed millionaire
stories.summary: Leftist Iván Cepeda conceded to far-right lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella who won by razor-thin margin The defeated leftwing candidate in Colombia’s presidential runoff has conceded to the far-right, Trump-admiring millionaire lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella. Since Sunday night, the preliminary count had already pointed to a De la Espriella victory by a razor-thin margin of less than 1% of the vote. Continue reading...

stories.title: AI helps read papyrus scroll burnt to crisp during Vesuvius eruption
stories.summary: Previously hidden text revealed without unrolling scroll discusses stoic philosophy on ethics, art and human behaviour The surviving part of an ancient scroll that was burnt to a crisp when Mount Vesuvius erupted nearly 2,000 years ago has been virtually unwrapped and read with help from artificial intelligence. Researchers uncovered 20 columns of previously hidden text covering more than a metre of charred papyrus without physically unrolling the scroll. The work discusses stoic philosophy on e

stories.title: Europe heatwave live: UK breaks temperature record for June as parts of France hit 40C – as it happened
stories.summary: West Sussex reports temperature of 35.8C, beating previous record from 1976; red weather alert extended to 72 of France’s 96 mainland departments Grahame Madge, a Met Office spokesperson, said the agency is forecasting 39C as a headline maximum temperature on Thursday in the UK, most likely for somewhere in London or the south-east. “It is possible we could see temperatures higher than the 39C if the final values are at the upper end of our narrow range,” he said, according to the Press Associat

stories.title: Big tech spent millions on a single US congressional race. It won’t be the last time
stories.summary: Pro- and anti-AI groups spent $24m on a congressional contest in New York, but it’s unclear to what end US politics live – latest updates When the Democratic primary for New York ’s 12th congressional district was called on Tuesday night, the result capped off one of the most expensive races of its kind in the state’s history. More than $24m poured into the Manhattan contest from tech-backed financial groups as the campaign turned into a battleground for pro- and anti- AI groups to test their in

stories.title: Member of cultlike Zizians group charged with murder of her parents
stories.summary: Michelle Zajko, in jail since February 2025 on other charges, has been charged in 2022 Pennsylvania killings of parents A member of the cultlike group known as Zizians, who has denied killing her parents in Pennsylvania in 2022, has been charged with murder, a prosecutor said on Wednesday. Michelle Zajko, who has been jailed in Maryland on other charges since February 2025, has been charged with murder, burglary and conspiracy charges in the deaths of Rita and Richard Zajko, the Delaware county

stories.title: Exploit last North Sea oil and gas or risk mass job losses, Andy Burnham urged
stories.summary: British Chamber of Commerce boss Shevaun Haviland says transition to clean energy could be handled better Andy Burnham should be ready to exploit the UK’s remaining North Sea oil and gas resources to avoid mass job losses in Scotland and the north-east, the director of the British Chambers of Commerce (BCC), Shevaun Haviland, has said. The decision about whether to allow extraction at the Jackdaw and Rosebank fields now appears likely to fall to a Burnham administration. Continue reading...

stories.title: UK records its hottest June day and France its hottest day ever as heatwave sweeps Europe
stories.summary: Temperature of 36.1C (97F) recorded in Hampshire, while two-thirds of Europe’s population experience temperatures above 30C The UK has broken its all-time temperature record for June and France has recorded its hottest day ever for the second day running, as a heatwave affecting more than 90 million people sweeps across swathes of Europe. As the UK and France registered record-breaking temperatures, the World Health Organization warned that the extreme temperatures are “putting lives at risk”. C

stories.title: Trump under pressure to back up claim of sabotage at reflecting pool
stories.summary: President’s promise of photo and video evidence of vandalism at Washington landmark yet to be fulfilled Donald Trump and the Department of the Interior are facing growing pressure to release photo and video evidence substantiating their claims of sabotage at the Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool in Washington. The $14.7m renovation of the landmark has descended into a farce of algae blooms, peeling paint and dead ducks just days before the US’s 250th anniversary celebrations. Crews have been seen

stories.title: Trump accused of showing ‘complete indifference’ to Americans’ living costs after cancelling housing bill signing – US politics live
stories.summary: President was due to sign deal aimed at making life more affordable for Americans but shelved event to pressure Congress to pass restrictive voting bill Trump abruptly cancels signing of bill aimed at lowering housing cost Sign up for the Breaking News US email A man or a movement? That was the question being asked when Zohran Mamdani gambled his political capital on Tuesday’s elections in New York . The answer from voters was emphatic: they prefer Mamdani and his brand of democratic socialism t

stories.title: ‘Horrific’ maternity care failings at Nottingham NHS trust prompt calls for public inquiry
stories.summary: Report uncovers biggest childbirth scandal in NHS history in which 520 mothers and babies suffered ‘potentially avoidable’ harm or died Horrific failings led to 520 mothers and babies in Nottingham suffering harm or dying, sparking calls for a public inquiry into maternity care across England. In all, 444 women and 76 newborn babies suffered “potentially avoidable” outcomes, a damning three-year long review of the biggest childbirth scandal in NHS history concluded. A “bullying and toxic culture

stories.title: Keir Starmer couldn’t beat the curse of Brexit – a politics poisoned by nationalism | Rafael Behr
stories.summary: The outgoing prime minister’s efforts to mobilise a healthier kind of patriotism fell flat. Andy Burnham may stand a better chance Britain is not ungovernable, but the chalice of high office has been spiked with unusually fast-acting poison. Six prime ministers down in a decade. The spectacle of the lectern planted outside No 10 for a resignation speech has acquired the familiarity of ritual. Since the Brexit referendum, the average tenure in Downing Street has been less than two years. That bal

stories.title: Why Austrian author Ingeborg Bachmann remains a literary icon
stories.summary: A new documentary featuring Sandra Hüller celebrates the Austrian poet and author who was a literary star of her era. She would have turned 100 this year.

stories.title: Iran nuclear inspections 'going to happen,' says IAEA head
stories.summary: After contradicting US and Iranian statements, the UN nuclear agency said inspections of Iran's nuclear sites would take place. Iran's top negotiator called the US-Iran deal "America's declaration of defeat." More at DW.

stories.title: India news: Protests in northern region Ladakh over talks with Delhi
stories.summary: Businesses in both northern Leh and Kargil districts remained shut to protest the alleged retraction of key constitutional and administrative agreements. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Southeast Asia boost defense ties but avoids firm alliances
stories.summary: Countries across the region are expanding defense cooperation in ways that change the South China Sea equation but fall short of a new anti-China bloc.

stories.title: Is xenophobia in South Africa risking investment?
stories.summary: Vigilante groups and right-wing populist forces are stoking anti-immigrant sentiment and demanding that they leave the country by June 30. Experts fear serious economic implications for the country and the region.

stories.title: France reports first Congo outbreak linked Ebola case
stories.summary: France has confirmed its first Ebola case linked to the outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The infection comes weeks after European health authorities said there were no active Ebola cases in the EU.

stories.title: Climate change: Heatwave costs the German economy billions
stories.summary: Never before has a June been as hot in Germany as in 2026. This has consequences not only for people and nature, but also for businesses. Heat reduces productivity and causes illness, while cooling is expensive.

stories.title: European heat wave is not normal summer weather
stories.summary: Human-induced climate change has intensified temperatures, making this year's record heat wave up to 4 degrees Celsius hotter.

stories.title: Heat wave: Why you should skip that cold beer or cocktail
stories.summary: Summer, the FIFA World Cup and a cold beer. Sounds like a dream? Be careful, though; the combination of heat and alcohol can quickly take a very bad turn.

stories.title: How Big Tech is changing journalism
stories.summary: Technology companies are shaping how news is presented and digested, leaving media outlets little option but to comply. At DW's Global Media Forum in Bonn, participants discussed how to resist and adapt.

stories.title: Dead tree painted red to highlight climate change impact
stories.summary: A painted red oak at Kew Gardens, killed in the 2022 heatwave, stands beside a resilient “Tree of Hope”, highlighting climate change impacts in the UK.

stories.title: French woman allegedly held captive by husband for 12 years rescued in Pakistan
stories.summary: Yasmina alleges she was held along with her five children, and they were all cut off from the outside world.

stories.title: Kazakhstan signs €10bn in deals with Europe as Tokayev wraps Brussels visit
stories.summary: During President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev's visit to Brussels, strategic documents were signed with the EU, and 30 commercial deals were concluded with EU companies, with the EU being the Central Asian country's largest trading and investment partner.

stories.title: Why are there holiday delay warnings over the EU's new border system?
stories.summary: The EU's much-delayed Entry/Exit System will change the way UK passengers travel to 29 countries.

stories.title: Giesinger celebrates Munich beer culture, pushes for Oktoberfest place
stories.summary: Munich Brewers’ Day 2026 brings processions, ceremony and beer celebrations to Marienplatz and Viktualienmarkt, honouring the city’s brewing guild tradition.

stories.title: Evo Morales warns of civil war from Chapare stronghold: 'I will not surrender'
stories.summary: Evo Morales speaks out from hiding in the Chapare jungle as Bolivia enters its seventh week of fuel, food and medicine shortages.

stories.title: Clean sweep for Mamdani-backed candidates in New York's Democratic primary
stories.summary: Brad Lander unseats Dan Goldman in a race that laid bare the city's divisions over the Israel-Gaza war.

stories.title: Air conditioning creates political divide after France records hottest day
stories.summary: France is being forced to re-think its longstanding reservations about air-conditioning as temperatures soar.

stories.title: Tehran labels framework deal 'US defeat' as Trump threatens to halt talks
stories.summary: Iranian key negotiator hailed the US–Iran framework deal as a “declaration of US defeat,” while Trump warned that talks would end immediately if reports that Tehran is seeking to impose tolls on ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz prove to be true.

stories.title: Kenya to charge students with murder over deadly school fire
stories.summary: Sixteen pupils, aged between 15 and 18, died when a fire broke out in a dormitory at Utumishi Girls' School last month.

stories.title: First whales killed as Iceland restarts commercial hunt
stories.summary: Iceland resumes commercial whaling after a two-year pause, with two fin whales processed at the Hvalur facility, reigniting controversy and protests.

stories.title: Congress passes war powers measure for first time, breaking with Trump over Iran
stories.summary: Experts say Trump is likely to ignore it, but that even though it carries little power, it is politically significant.

stories.title: France confirms first Ebola case
stories.summary: The French doctor had been working in DR Congo, where more than 260 people are known to have died.

stories.title: Portugal's World Cup victory sparks celebrations in Lisbon
stories.summary: Thousands of fans in Lisbon celebrated Portugal's 5-0 World Cup victory over Uzbekistan, with Cristiano Ronaldo scoring twice and becoming the first player to score in six different World Cup tournaments.

stories.title: IMF to closely monitor China’s economic growth amid trade tensions
stories.summary: An accurate measurement of China's digital economy is a priority for other powers competing with Beijing in fast-developing technologies.

stories.title: Power outages hit France as it records hottest day since measurements began
stories.summary: France has recorded it hottest day since records began in 1947, its national weather agency says, breaking a record set on Tuesday.

stories.title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
stories.summary: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.

stories.title: Catholics walk barefoot in mud ritual
stories.summary: Devotees in rice paddies cover themselves in mud and banana leaves for the Taong Putik festival, honouring St. John the Baptist with barefoot processions.

stories.title: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba sues US government over defence blacklist
stories.summary: It is suing the US defence department after it was added to a list of firms with ties to the Chinese military.

stories.title: Eight sentenced to 450 years in prison over anti-ICE riot where officer was shot
stories.summary: An officer was shot in the neck during the Texas disorder by "Antifa Cell operatives", prosecutors said.

stories.title: Kenyan minister orders halt to construction of US Ebola facility
stories.summary: Decision comes after Aden Duale was held in contempt for ignoring previous high court ruling to stop work Kenya’s health minister told a court he had ordered preparations for a US-run Ebola quarantine facility to stop, after being held in contempt for ignoring a previous order to end work. Many Kenyans strongly oppose the facility , with deadly protests erupting since the complex was announced in May for US citizens evacuated from the Democratic Republic of Congo, which is grappling with a wides

stories.title: Turkey arrests more than 200 in crackdown before NATO summit
stories.summary: The Ankara prosecutor's office said 209 people were detained, while 32 others remained at large. Turkey has announced a ban on demonstrations amid preparations for next month's NATO summit in the capital.

stories.title: Kenya orders immediate halt to controversial US-backed Ebola facility
stories.summary: The project had sparked court proceedings, public protests and questions about the country's role in responding to an outbreak beyond its borders.

stories.title: Play puts spotlight on Kenya’s crisis of gender-based violence
stories.summary: Autobiographical work Free Me aims to encourage victims to speak out in country where violence against women is rising There are audible gasps in the auditorium in Nairobi as a husband launches a volley of blows and slaps on his wife and pushes her to the floor. “I wish I could spare you this,” the wife tells the audience. “My husband beat me up as if we were in a bar fight. Except, in a bar someone fights back.” The scene comes from Free Me, an autobiographical play by Gathoni Kimuyu, a Kenyan

stories.title: Dettol apologises after ‘toxic men’ advert sparks backlash in China
stories.summary: British disinfectant brand withdraws advert about a man’s efforts to find a ‘clean and untouched’ woman The British hygiene brand Dettol has apologised after an advertisement released in China, which it said was intended to criticise “toxic men”, was widely condemned on social media as offensive to women. The five-minute advert for a multipurpose disinfectant, released across many online platforms at the end of May, features a man comparing his girlfriend with his former partner. Learning that h

stories.title: US: Google's YouTube settles teen mental health lawsuit
stories.summary: A Florida teenager said YouTube's addictive design contributed to depression, anxiety and sleep loss. Instagram, Snapchat and TikTok are also facing similar allegations about their impact on young users.

stories.title: US: Senate vote calls for an end to Trump's Iran war in rare bipartisan rebuke
stories.summary: Trump criticized the largely symbolic Senate resolution as "meaningless" after several Republicans joined Democrats in a rare break with the White House.

stories.title: How Africa's youth wants to save democracy
stories.summary: Africa's young population often finds itself facing aging, autocratic rulers. Unwilling to settle for democratic rituals alone, many are searching for creative new ways to shape their future.

stories.title: Giant Messi statue honors Argentina's World Cup hero
stories.summary: A giant mural near Buenos Aires and a towering statue in Patagonia highlight Lionel Messi's enduring status as a symbol of unity and pride for Argentines during the World Cup.

stories.title: Airbus under fresh scrutiny as EASA orders A380 inspections over wing cracks
stories.summary: The aviation giant is already battling supply chain problems and growing costs across its operations.

stories.title: Temperatures hit record levels in western Europe
stories.summary: France, Spain and Italy, have been hardest hit by the heatwave so far.

stories.title: From startups to global spotlight - Indian entrepreneur takes charge of WhatsApp
stories.summary: Kunal Shah has been a recognisable figure in India's startup ecosystem for a while but now he faces global spotlight.

stories.title: Stanford graduates rethink their futures as AI transforms tech
stories.summary: The BBC spoke with Stanford University graduates about what they really think about artificial intelligence.

stories.title: Germany rail network briefly halted nationwide due to IT malfunction
stories.summary: Rail company Deutsche Bahn had to pause train services across the country for more than two-and-a-half hours.

stories.title: Sydney woman wakes from induced coma more than a week after shark attack
stories.summary: Leah Stewart, 34, had one of her arms amputated after she was bitten while swimming at Coogee Beach.

stories.title: Bondi Beach shooting hero pleads not guilty to alleged assault on his father
stories.summary: Ahmed al Ahmed tackled one of the gunmen who killed 15 people at a Jewish event in December.

stories.title: New giraffe born in Denmark zoo
stories.summary: At Copenhagen Zoo, a giraffe calf is born as staff and visitors witness the moment. The mother immediately cares for her newborn.

stories.title: Toronto streets fill with Panama and Croatia supporters
stories.summary: Supporters of Panama and Croatia turned downtown Toronto into a sea of flags and chants ahead of their crucial World Cup Group L clash.

stories.title: IAEA chief says inspections of Iran's nuclear sites 'going to happen'
stories.summary: IAEA's Grossi pushed back against Tehran's claim that no agreement has been reached to allow inspectors to inspect bombed enrichment sites, citing the signed US-Iran framework deal.

stories.title: Még egy héttel meghosszabbították a vörös kódot a hőkupolával érkező extrém hőség miatt
stories.summary: Hétvégére a 40 fok is megérkezik, csütörtöktől az egész ország területére tűzgyújtási tilalmat rendeltek el.

stories.title: Megszűnik a Fidesz soproni szócsöve, a Soproni Téma is
stories.summary: Sem online, sem nyomtatásban nem jelenik meg a jövőben, és a Facebook-oldalt is megszüntetik.

stories.title: Bús Balázzsal együtt azokat vették őrizetbe, akik döntöttek az NKA 17 milliárdos támogatásairól
stories.summary: A Telex információja szerint a Nemzeti Kulturális Alap pályázatkezelő szervének főigazgatója, kabinetfőnöke és a támogatásokról döntő három bizottsági tag van őrizetben. A nyomozás szálai Bács-Kiskun vármegyéig vezettek. Az illetékes ügyészség ma délután dönt arról, hogy indítványozza-e a gyanúsítottak letartóztatását.

stories.title: Megdőlt a negatív rekord, sosem volt ilyen alacsony a Velencei-tó vízállása
stories.summary: A mostani rekord várhatóan többször is megdől majd a nyár folyamán.

stories.title: Azonnali hatállyal felmondta a Nemzeti Rendezvényszervező Ügynökség a Balásy-cégekkel kötött keretszerződéseit
stories.summary: Az ügynökséget felügyelő Belügyminisztérium a negatív körülményekkel és a szerződések teljesíthetőségével összefüggésben felmerült súlyos kockázatokkal indokolta a döntést.

stories.title: Zászlókkal emlékezett a lebombázott iráni iskola áldozataira a fociválogatott
stories.summary: 168 gyerek halt meg a minabi iskola lebombázásakor.

stories.title: Megváltoztatják az áramhasználatunkat a fejlesztések, amikre a kormány 500 milliárdot szán
stories.summary: Egyre nagyobb potenciál van a megújulók hatékonyabb felhasználásában az energiatárolás terjedésével. Ebben az okosmérők is segíthetnek, amelyekből itthon még kevés van, de lesz rájuk pályázati pénz.

stories.title: A TASZ szerint ki kell venni a mostani Alaptörvény-módosításból a 12 éves képviselői mandátumlimitet
stories.summary: Álláspontjuk szerint ebben az ügyben nem indokolt a sietség, és érdemi társadalmi vitát nem lehet folytatni egy ötnapos társadalmi egyeztetés keretében.

stories.title: A 39 éves Dzsudzsák Balázs még egy szezonra aláírt a DVSC-hez
stories.summary: A futballista korábban azt mondta, hogy az életkor csak egy szám.

stories.title: Kiderült, mennyi pénzt kell majd kicsengetni a GTA VI-ért
stories.summary: A Mario Kart World után ez lesz a második nagy videójáték, aminek az alapverziója 80 dollárba fog kerülni.

stories.title: Emléktáblát és padot állíthatnak Scherer Péter emlékére a Margit-szigeten
stories.summary: A héten szavaz a Fővárosi Közgyűlés a Fidesz képviselőjének javaslatáról.

stories.title: 81 éves szörföst mentettek ki a Balatonból a vízirendőrök
stories.summary: A férfi azt mondta a rendőröknek, nagyon kimerült, ami miatt képtelen volt partközelbe jutni.

stories.title: Először találtak ebolával fertőzött beteget Franciaországban
stories.summary: Afrikából tért haza az orvos, aki elkapta a Kongóban terjedő betegséget.

stories.title: Picasso vitte a prímet az Art Baselen, de Richter és Hockney művei is taroltak
stories.summary: A világ egyik legfontosabb kortárs művészeti vásárán a legnagyobb üzlet egy Picasso-festményhez kapcsolódott, de Gerhard Richter és David Hockney alkotásaiért is több tízmillió dollárt fizettek a gyűjtők.

stories.title: Találtak valamit a nyíregyházi kórház vizében, korlátozták az ivóvízhasználatot több épületben
stories.summary: Nem megnevezett „mikrobiológiai eltéréseket” azonosítottak a vízminőségi ellenőrzések a kórház vízhálózatában.

stories.title: De szép kanapé! Kár, hogy tele van csempészcigivel
stories.summary: Röszkén kapták szét a bútort, 168 ezer szál koszovói zárjegyes cigaretta volt benne.

stories.title: Hogyhogy nem kapott pirosat Bellingham, miután eltakarta a száját Ghána ellen?
stories.summary: A paraguayi Miguel Almirónt kiállították, mert eltakarta a száját, amikor az ellenfélnek mondott valamit. Jude Bellingham Ghána ellen hasonlót csinált, ám megúszta.

stories.title: Ebben a könyvben ott van Esterházy, halljuk a hangját, látjuk a gesztusait, és ez valami nagyszerű dolog
stories.summary: A Vanni van beszélgetéseit folytatva újabb beszélgetőkönyv jelent meg Esterházy interjúiból szemlézve, ám most Esterházy „másról” beszél, azaz nem az irodalomról, hanem az egyebekről. Ami nyilván blöff, mert EP bármiről is beszél, az önmagában irodalom – írja a kötetről kritikusunk.

stories.title: Ronaldinho 46 évesen, az olasz harmadosztályban térhet vissza a pályára
stories.summary: A Ravenna tulajdonosa szerint nem csupán reklámfogásról van szó, az aranylabdás brazil focista legalább egy mérkőzésen pályára fog lépni a bajnokságban.

stories.title: Tilos jelzésen haladt át az érkező vonat a múlt heti angliai vasúti balesetben, több mint 100 ember kórházba került
stories.summary: A mozdonyvezető meghalt a balesetben, 162 ember sérült meg az ütközésben.

stories.title: Felfüggesztik a Semcorp debreceni gyárának tevékenységét
stories.summary: A kínai akkuipari cégtől vett minták határérték-túllépést mutattak.

stories.title: Befotózott a Tejútrendszer közepére a Euclid űrtávcső, több mint 60 millió csillagot talált
stories.summary: Csak 26 órán át szegezte lencséjét a galaxisunk közepére, de így is az eddigi legrészletesebb látható fényben készült fényképeket lőtte.

stories.title: Közleményt adott ki az Újpest FC elnöke a focistadion építésének szüneteltetéséről
stories.summary: Ratatics Péter szerint csak technikai döntésről van szó, de bizonytalan a szabályozói környezet is.

stories.title: A pokoli hőség letaglózta Európát, és nem is lesz hamar vége
stories.summary: Felforrósodott autóban meghalt gyerekek, felhevült testtel vízbe ugró, majd megfulladó emberek, alkoholtilalom. Franciaországban megdőlt a melegrekord, és már negyvenen meghaltak a hőségben. Egyre több országban küzdenek áramkimaradásokkal, vezetnek be korlátozásokat, ahogy nyugatról keletre húzódik egy hőkupola.

stories.title: A FIFA adatai szerint továbbra is telt házasak a meccsek, simán meg fog dőlni a nézettségi rekord
stories.summary: Az első 44 mérkőzésen 2,85 millió néző volt kint az amerikai, mexikói és kanadai stadionokban.

stories.title: Jelentősen romlott a vízminőség a narancssárgára váltott miskolci Szinva patakban
stories.summary: A halak jelentős része szerencsére el tudott úszni a szennyezéssel leginkább érintett területekről.

stories.title: Kapitány: Augusztus végén a kormány jelentős szélerőmű-építési pályázatot ír ki
stories.summary: Legalább 700 megawattnyi kapacitásbővítéssel terveznek.

stories.title: Állami programba szálltak be, durván megszívták: milliárdokat nem fizetett ki a Fidesz-kormány az egyik kkv-pályázat beszállítóinak
stories.summary: A kisebb cégek honlapfejlesztését célzó intézkedésre megvolt a pénz, és senki nem akarta leállítani, de a forrás csak nem jutott el a megvalósítókhoz. Abszurd történet bénázásról és az államigazgatás útvesztőiről.

stories.title: Bódis Kriszta: Mi volt a társadalompolitika? Ami a Karmelitát nem érdekli
stories.summary: Parlamenti pókerarcáról, a miniszterség elkerülésén át a kiégésig és Pócs Jánosig több témáról is beszélt a kormánybiztos Krug Emília Szinten túl című műsorában.

stories.title: Amíg a föld kerek, mindig lesznek sértett rockerek
stories.summary: Paul Rudd kiöregedett rocksztárként néz szembe azzal, hogy a daloknak ma nem karizmatikusnak, hanem ártalmatlannak kell lenniük. A millenniálok filmje lehetett volna, de sajnos klisékbe fullad John Carney, a Sing Street és a Once alkotójának új rendezése.

stories.title: Ruszin-Szendi az átműtött emberekről: Ha saját magával nincsen tisztában, akkor elég nehéz fegyvert adni a kezébe
stories.summary: A honvédelmi miniszter szerint egyszer jelentkezett katonának egy átműtött ember, de rájött, hogy „ezzel a fajta kultúrával” a haderőben nem tudnak mit kezdeni, el is ment magától.

stories.title: Zelenszkij szerint már nem működnek az orosz drónok által használt átjátszóállomások Belaruszban
stories.summary: Egyelőre nem világos, hogy a belarusz erők szerelték-e le az állomásokat.

Őrizetbe vették Fonyód volt fideszes polgármesterét
stories.summary: Az eljárás különösen nagy vagyoni hátrányt okozó hűtlen kezelés bűntettének gyanúja miatt indult.

stories.title: Augusztus 20-án nyit újra a két éve leégett Miskolctapolcai Barlangfürdő
stories.summary: „Szerelési hibák sorozata, valamint a karbantartás és a szabványossági felülvizsgálat hiánya vezetett” a tűzhöz a tűzvizsgálati jelentés szerint.

stories.title: Former Pinochet agents convicted over 1976 Washington DC carbomb murder
stories.summary: Attack targeted former Chile ambassador Orlando Letelier and his US colleague Ronni Karpen Moffitt Fifty years after Gen Augusto Pinochet ’s secret police detonated a car bomb in the heart of Washington DC , killing Orlando Letelier, a former Chilean minister and ambassador to the US, and his American colleague Ronni Karpen Moffitt, a Santiago court has convicted three former agents of Moffitt’s murder. Judge Paola Plaza, a special minister for human rights in Chile, sentenced Pedro Espinoza, Jo

stories.title: Fact-checking Brexit's key promises 10 years on – video
stories.summary: On 23 June 2016 Britons voted to leave the EU. A decade later, the Guardian's economics editor, Heather Stewart, looks back at the Brexit campaign promises. From immigration, a bus promising £350m a week for the NHS, to bendy bananas, the 'most consequential decision in modern history' had mixed results From bendy bananas to £350m for the NHS – how many Brexit promises actually came true? Europe’s media look on in bemusement at post-Brexit ‘revolving door’ of UK prime ministers Continue reading.

stories.summary: Prime minister said to have left Downing Street for secret meeting with his probable successor Burnham adviser calls for billions of pounds in borrowing for infrastructure Peter Walker is a senior Guardian political correspondent. The Liberal Democrats are marking the tenth anniversary of Brexit by enjoying their favourite pursuit – being rude about Nigel Farage. Nigel Farage pocketed a £5m “reward” for the damage he’s caused, while the rest of us are paying for it dearly. When he promised we wo

stories.summary: It’s 10 years since the Brexit vote – and it’s also another one of those weeks in British politics … you asked our political columnist questions about a Burnham government, rejoining the EU, and more – read the Q&A below DyvimTvar asks: How do you think Burnham will deal with Trump? More of the same or will he be looking at the likely result of the midterm elections in the US? Raf: Burnham’s foreign policy is very mysterious. He very rarely talks about the world beyond British shores, and ha

stories.title: Israel continues to commit genocide by targeting children in Gaza, UN inquiry finds
stories.summary: Independent report says by aiming at children Israel is undermining capacity of Palestinian people to exist Israel continues to commit genocide by deliberately targeting Palestinian children in Gaza, an ⁠independent UN inquiry has found. The report by the UN independent international commission of inquiry examined violations against Palestinian children since the start of the war in Gaza, and said about 30% of the people killed by Israeli forces have been children. Continue reading...

stories.title: Federal judge blocks Trump's push to use database to check citizenship status prior to voting
stories.summary: US federal court says the Trump-led initiative carries the risk of preventing valid voters from their constitutional right to democracy and tramples on Americans’ privacy.

stories.title: Von der Leyen to visit Pashinyan in Yerevan to build on Armenia’s pro-EU vote
stories.summary: As the EU rolls out a support package for Armenia, European Commission President von der Leyen is set to meet re-elected Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan in Yerevan after he secured a decisive pro-West mandate from the Armenian people, sources confirmed to Euronews.

stories.title: Fact check: AI fakes around the World Cup spread political narratives
stories.summary: Keir Starmer in a Croatia jersey. An Iranian protest against a US strike. Calls to arrest Brazil's President Lula. Adolf Hitler in the German fan section — all supposedly seen at the World Cup. But none of it is real.

stories.title: Lost memoir of Hiroshima survivor found after decades in US archive
stories.summary: Written in 1947, Kiyoshi Tanimoto’s account of the horrors of the atomic bomb attack will be published in August and is being made into a film The memoir of a man who survived the horrors of Hiroshima is to be published for the first time this summer after its discovery in a US archive. The 230-page memoir was written almost 80 years ago by Kiyoshi Tanimoto, who witnessed the city’s destruction after the atomic bomb was dropped in 1945. He will now be portrayed in a feature film by Takehiro Hira

stories.title: Whoosh! Jacques Henri Lartigue’s world of colour – in pictures
stories.summary: The French artist is best known for his black-and-white images of high society, but a new exhibition shows us that he was also a bold and brilliant colour pioneer Continue reading...

stories.title: Europe’s June heatwave – in pictures
stories.summary: The ‘heat dome’ settling over western Europe could bring temperatures of up to 40C to some parts of England and Wales in the middle part of this week Continue reading...

stories.title: EU faces fierce criticism over plans to host Taliban in Brussels
stories.summary: Rights campaigners and MEPs say meeting would normalise regime that erases women from public life Rights campaigners and MEPs have warned that a meeting between EU officials and a Taliban delegation in Brussels risks normalising a regime that has banned girls from school beyond the sixth grade and sought to erase women from public life, while its ranks include two leaders accused of crimes against humanity. A spokesperson for the Afghan foreign ministry confirmed that a delegation representing t

stories.title: Europe battles record-breaking heat: is this the new normal? - The Latest
stories.summary: Europe is dealing with a debilitating heatwave, with schools closed, trains cancelled and France holding an emergency meeting after heat-related deaths. António Guterres, the UN chief, is urging the world to act on fossil fuels as the continent braces for record-breaking heat. Lucy Hough speaks to Europe environment correspondent Ajit Niranjan Continue reading...

stories.title: Rubio insists strait of Hormuz will be toll-free as he arrives for Gulf meeting
stories.summary: US secretary of state seeks to reassure UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain over security and US-Iran ceasefire deal The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, has said no country, including Iran, would be allowed to charge tolls for shipping in the strait of Hormuz as he sought to reassure US allies in the Gulf that Washington would take a firm line in peace negotiations with Tehran. Rubio is to meet Gulf allies on Tuesday and Wednesday in an attempt to reassure them that the US remains committed to their sec

stories.title: World Cup: Amiri another wildcard for Nagelsmann's Germany
stories.summary: The Mainz midfielder with Afghan roots has become a key member of Germany's 2026 World Cup squad. Coach Julian Nagelsmann said Amiri can provide the team with a spark when it's most needed.

stories.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
stories.summary: A 33-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, but opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.

stories.title: More than a unibrow: The enduring appeal of Frida Kahlo
stories.summary: She shunned feminine beauty ideals, was bisexual and a political radical, all while being brutally honest in her art. A major Frida Kahlo exhibition opens at London's Tate Modern.

stories.title: Germany news: Merz hails pension reform proposals
stories.summary: Chancellor Merz says a proposed pension reform package should be fully implemented. A survey has shown that almost half of teachers in Germany find pupils' behavior stressful.

stories.title: Europe is spending billions to rearm, so why the delays?
stories.summary: European countries have been trying to scale up their military capabilities, but their current defense production and procurement model is not fit for purpose, say experts.

stories.title: Experts fired by Trump resurrect mothballed climate website
stories.summary: Fired US federal workers have revived a defunct climate website — pushing back as the Trump administration escalates cuts to publicly funded science and research.

stories.title: Scotland: Sturgeon's ex-husband Peter Murrell jailed for 5 years and 3 months
stories.summary: Peter Murrell pleaded guilty to embezzling funds in his role as chief executive of the Scottish National Party. He held the SNP's purse strings while his then-wife, Nicola Sturgeon, was Scottish First Minister.

stories.title: Belgium issues visas to Taliban delegation for EU migration talks
stories.summary: The EU is preparing to host Taliban representatives for the first time since the group's return to power in Afghanistan.

stories.title: Honduras to buy Ukrainian drones to fight drug trafficking
stories.summary: The discussions come as the Central American country continues to grapple with drug trafficking and some of the highest levels of violence in the region.

stories.title: Vance says direct talks with Iran set 'good foundation' for deal to end the war
stories.summary: Lower-level officials are continuing to engage in what officials described as “technical talks” in Switzerland following Vance, Ghalibaf and Araghchi’s departure, as the US and Iran look to inch closer to a concrete deal.

stories.title: Kazakhstan brings New Silk Road revival to Brussels as Tokayev visits EU leaders
stories.summary: Kazakhstan's president has arrived in Brussels this week to meet EU leaders and assess the delivery of promises made at last year's EU-Central Asia summit in Samarkand, with the Middle Corridor trade route connecting China to Europe through Central Asia at the top of the agenda.

stories.title: Why was JD Vance in a luxury Swiss resort for talks with Iran?
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Jessica Parker explains some key points of the direct talks between the US vice-president and Iran.

stories.title: Nancy Guthrie ransom note said she died after abduction
stories.summary: Guthrie went missing from her home in Tucson, Arizona, in early February. The FBI is offering a reward up to $100,000 for information leading to her location.

stories.title: Brussels residents cool off in fountains as heatwave grips Belgium
stories.summary: People flocked to fountains in Brussels' Parc du Cinquantenaire as temperatures climbed during a heatwave expected to intensify later this week.

stories.title: Police officer and civilian killed in Montreal shooting
stories.summary: Montreal Police named the officer killed in the shooting as 34-year-old Mohamed Lamine Benredouane.

stories.title: Watch: French jets salute US anniversary with Washington flyover
stories.summary: France's elite Patrouille de France aerobatic team performed a ceremonial flyover above some of Washington's most iconic sites as part of celebrations marking 250 years of American independence.

stories.title: Alpine glacier melts faster as second heatwave hits France
stories.summary: The Bossons Glacier in France’s Chamonix Valley is showing fresh signs of rapid retreat as a second heatwave of 2026 grips parts of Europe.

stories.title: TV pundit apologises after racist comment during Belgium World Cup game
stories.summary: Bogdanović most notably played for La Liga side Atlético Madrid and Germany's Werder Bremen during his career.

stories.title: 40 mayors worldwide unite to fight back against the AI data centre surge
stories.summary: From Phoenix to Melbourne, a global coalition of city leaders says the AI data centre boom is draining their power, water and land — and they are done waiting.

stories.title: Iran sharply contradicts the US, pushing its own view of framework deal
stories.summary: Shortly after the first high-level US-Iran negotiations ended, discrepancies emerged over what the two sides actually agreed, as Tehran moved to directly contradict US Vice President JD Vance’s statements, prompting US President Donald Trump to reject Iran's claims.

stories.title: Fire at animation studio in India kills at least 15 people
stories.summary: The fire happened in a building housing a student training centre in northern India, raising fresh concerns about fire safety in the country.

stories.title: From cool-down spots to chalk on windows - how Europe is coping with the heat
stories.summary: People across the continent are taking measures to cope with the searing temperatures gripping Europe.

stories.title: Dozens of ships head through Strait of Hormuz after US-Iran deal
stories.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

stories.title: Armenia and Azerbaijan agree landmark internet deal as peace process continues
stories.summary: Armenia and Azerbaijan’s leading telecom companies agreed to a joint internet transit project as both countries move forward together after decades of war, boosting regional connectivity.

stories.title: From Bolsonaro and Bukele to Fujimori and De la Espriella: Trump and Latin America's rightward turn
stories.summary: Soon after the US president first moved into the White House, his policies and outspoken backing for certain candidates inspired a new wave of Latin American leaders now shaping the continent’s future.

stories.title: 'We're begging' - Savannah Guthrie pleads for help as details of her mother’s case emerge
stories.summary: Savannah Guthrie spoke on NBC's Today show about a note sent to the media in February regarding her missing mother, Nancy Guthrie, which claimed she had died.

stories.title: UN launches Hormuz evacuation for 11,000 stranded seafarers
stories.summary: The operation follows a peace agreement between the United States and Iran that ended months of conflict and allowed commercial shipping to resume through one of the world's most important maritime routes.

stories.title: Japan's emperor and empress arrive in Belgium for state visit
stories.summary: The arrival of Japan's emperor and empress in Belgium marks a symbolic moment for two countries whose partnership stretches back more than a century and a half.

stories.title: UNAIDS warns Trump's HIV funding cuts to South Africa could cost lives
stories.summary: The Trump administration says the phased withdrawal is linked to policy disputes with Pretoria, which rejects the allegations and says it is pursuing greater self-reliance.

stories.title: Belgian royals roll out red carpet for Japan's emperor
stories.summary: Japanese Emperor Naruhito and Empress Masako received a ceremonial welcome at the Royal Palace of Brussels as they began a state visit to Belgium.

stories.title: Why is Bulgaria threatening to veto EU's Russia sanctions?
stories.summary: Bulgarian PM Rumen Radev has vowed to veto the EU's latest sanctions against Russia because they include Russian Orthodox Patriarch Kirill and former Lukoil president Vagit Alekperov. What's behind his opposition?

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: Deniz Undav: Germany's 2026 World Cup supersub
stories.summary: With three goals in two games, striker Deniz Undav is the man in focus for Germany at the World Cup. But things could have turned out very differently for the Stuttgart striker.
